pages.raw_text:
Councilman Gray this morning
stated that he will never consent to
the Northern Pacific grade separa-
ton plan if Browne st. is to be
closed He said that the city had
fought two years to open the street
and since then expensive business
buildings have been erected south
of the tracks because there was an
outlet by Browne st. to the north.


Regarding the proposition that
the railroad company could buy up
that property Mr.. Gray stated that
he did not believe the company
would do so, for the reason That 1t
would too expensive an undertake
ing.


The grade separation plans will
be submitted to the body of council
men Tuesday night, at which time
it is said n P. counsel, Edward j..
Cannon, will offer what comprom-
Use, if any, the railroad company is
willing to make.
